name: Niue

colors.symbolism: the bright sunshine of Niue and the warm friendship between Niue and New Zealand
the dark blue of the Union Flag canton
the cross of St George and the saltire of St Patrick within the Union Flag
the saltire and cross fimbriations of the Union Flag, and the four small stars of the Southern Cross added on the canton

description: A yellow field bearing the Union Flag in the upper-hoist canton; on the canton's centre is a large yellow five-pointed star and four smaller yellow stars of the Southern Cross around it.
history: Adopted on 15 October 1975, just before Niue achieved self-government in free association with New Zealand on 19 October 1974. The flag's distinctive bright yellow field is unusual among Commonwealth-derived flags; the addition of yellow stars onto the Union Flag canton (rather than placing them on the field) is also unique.

features.symbolism: a deep golden-yellow field said to symbolise the bright sunshine of Niue and the warmth of the Niuean people's affection towards New Zealand and its people

features.role: Union Flag with five yellow stars added
features.symbolism: the Union Flag denotes Niue's continuing constitutional ties to the British Crown through New Zealand; the additional stars are unique to Niue's flag
Niue itself, self-governing within its free association with New Zealand

features.role: four smaller yellow stars in the form of the Southern Cross
features.symbolism: the Southern Cross, recalling Niue's place in the Southern Hemisphere and its links with New Zealand
